project_name: Reduced-Planner Scratchpad Feedback Real Repo-QA Validation

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this validation as positive; if more budget is allocated later, replicate the same harness on a second large repository/model rather than creating a generic successor branch.
decision.stop_reason: The real-repository atomic and paired-evidence live runs met the >=15% all-in prompt-like savings gate at equal or better answer quality, especially with amortized first-pass planning, and the branch-specific kill condition is not supported.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this validation as positive; if more budget is allocated later, replicate the same harness on a second large repository/model rather than creating a generic successor branch.
run_notes_tail: # Reduced-Planner Scratchpad Feedback Real Repo-QA Validation Run Notes
## 2026-04-20 initial execution plan
- Reviewed project directory: only OMX/prompt scaffolding exists; executable artifacts need to be acquired from the parent project or reconstructed here.

- Branch-specific hypothesis: the query-only, 6-token hidden planner should preserve equal answer quality while reducing all-in prompt-like tokens by at least 15% on larger real-repo QA/extraction tasks, especially when one first-pass planner call is amortized across multiple related questions.

- Branch-specific kill condition: finalize negative if a real-repo pilot with at least 20 live QA/extraction trials shows no cost-normalized equal-quality match with >=15% all-in token savings for either per-question or amortized planner accounting, or if gains appear only on saturated/low-quality baselines that do not exercise retrieval.

- Acquire/copy the parent harness patterns, build a new real-repo benchmark over public/local repository files with deterministic answer labels from actual source/docs, and include grouped question sets so one first-pass plan can be shared across multiple downstream questions.
- Run py_compile plus a small live smoke, then a bounded Qwen2.5-7B run with throughput/utilization metrics; update durable result artifacts and `.omx/project_decision.json` before yielding.
## 2026-04-20 real-repo validation completed

- Acquired parent harness primitives into this project and added `scripts/real_repo_qa_feedback_benchmark.py`, a live OpenAI-compatible benchmark that builds QA/extraction labels from actual local repository files instead of synthetic templates.
- Verification: `python3 -m py_compile scripts/real_repo_qa_feedback_benchmark.py scripts/scratchpad_context_benchmark.py` passed before live runs.
- Smoke: `results/real_repo_qwen7b_smoke` completed 4 cases / 24 live answer trials against `[redacted-local-llama.cpp-checkout]` with Qwen2.5-7B GGUF and confirmed server/run cleanup.

- Atomic real-repo run: `results/real_repo_qwen7b_reduced_planner` completed 24 cases across 8 source-file groups and 288 answer trials. The amortized 6-token planner matched vanilla 190-token and 260-token baselines with feedback budget 90, all-in prompt-like deltas -38.6% and -58.2% respectively; per-question planning also met the >=15% all-in savings gate on those baselines. At the tight 90/130 baselines, fixed planner overhead still prevented all-in savings despite equal or near-equal quality.

- Hard paired-evidence run: `results/real_repo_qwen7b_pair_hard` completed 16 paired cases across 8 groups and 192 answer trials, requiring two source facts per answer. Amortized feedback improved equal-budget F1 by +24.7 points at 90 tokens and +10.2 points at 130 tokens, with evidence-recall gains of +62.5 and +65.6 points. Cost-normalized matches met the success gate: vanilla 190 F1 0.953 matched by amortized feedback 90 F1 0.969 with -18.9% all-in prompt-like tokens; vanilla 260 F1 0.974 matched by amortized feedback 130 F1 0.984 with -31.0% all-in tokens. Per-question feedback also met the gate for 190/260 baselines, though amortization was cheaper.

- Performance/utilization: Qwen2.5-7B live answer calls averaged roughly 39-42 completion tok/s. Atomic run CPU mean ~5.56%, GPU mean ~95.8%, RSS ~0.75-2.07 GB, MemAvailable after run 115.6 GB. Pair run CPU mean ~5.55%, GPU mean ~95.7%, RSS ~0.76-1.81 GB, MemAvailable after run 115.9 GB. No helper `llama-server` process remained after completion.

- Interpretation: the reduced query-only/6-token hidden planner survived a larger real-repository validation and the branch-specific kill condition is not supported. The effect is strongest when vanilla needs larger context to recover multiple source facts and when the first pass is amortized across related questions; tiny baselines remain dominated by fixed planner overhead, and saturated single-fact cases are less informative even though they show cost savings against larger vanilla contexts.
- Durable artifacts:
- `scripts/real_repo_qa_feedback_benchmark.py`
- `results/real_repo_qwen7b_reduced_planner/live_summary.json`, `live_report.md`, `live_trial_results.csv`, `cases.json`, `scratchpads.json`, `llama_server.log`
- `results/real_repo_qwen7b_pair_hard/live_summary.json`, `live_report.md`, `live_trial_results.csv`, `cases.json`, `scratchpads.json`, `llama_server.log`
